raw_text:
CloudPulse Systems

Making infrastructure invisible, reliability visible.

JUNIOR SITE RELIABILITY ENGINEER

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly) · Full-time · $85,000 - $115,000 per year

Join CloudPulse Systems as a Junior Site Reliability Engineer and kickstart your career in

infrastructure reliability. You'll work alongside experienced SREs to design, deploy, and maintain our

production systems, learn industry best practices in observability and incident response, and

contribute to building infrastructure that serves thousands of users. This is an ideal role if you're

passionate about learning how systems work at scale and want hands-on experience with modern

cloud-native tooling.

ABOUT US

CloudPulse Systems is a fast-growing infrastructure software company founded in 2019, specializing

in cloud-native observability and reliability solutions. We empower engineering teams at mid-market
and enterprise companies to build and maintain resilient systems at scale. Our platform helps

thousands of developers monitor distributed systems, reduce incidents, and achieve their reliability
goals.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Assist in designing and implementing monitoring solutions using Prometheus and Grafana to

track system health and performance metrics

Participate in on-call rotation (with mentorship) to respond to production incidents, learning

incident response procedures and post-incident review practices

Write and maintain infrastructure-as-code using Terraform to provision and manage cloud

resources in a repeatable manner

Contribute to SLO/SLI definition and tracking for critical services, helping to establish reliability
targets and measure system performance

Collaborate with backend and platform teams to optimize application performance and
troubleshoot production issues

Learn and implement distributed tracing using tools like Jaeger to understand system behavior
and identify bottlenecks

Develop automation scripts in Python or Go to reduce toil and improve operational efficiency

Document runbooks, troubleshooting guides, and operational procedures to improve team
knowledge sharing

REQUIRED

 Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field, or equivalent

professional experience

Foundational understanding of Linux/Unix systems and command-line tools

Basic knowledge of containerization and orchestration concepts (Docker, Kubernetes

fundamentals)

Familiarity with at least one programming language (Python, Go, Bash, or similar) for scripting

and automation

Strong communication skills and ability to work well in a team environment

Enthusiasm for learning and willingness to take on new technical challenges

NICE TO HAVE

Experience with Kubernetes in development or personal projects

Familiarity with observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, or similar platforms

Previous exposure to incident response or on-call environments

Understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure)

TECH STACK

Kubernetes   Prometheus   Grafana   Jaeger (distributed tracing)

  Python   Go   Terraform   Docker

  Linux

SLO/SLI frameworks

BENEFITS

Competitive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage

401(k) matching up to 4% of salary

Unlimited paid time off with a minimum of 15 days encouraged per year

Professional development budget of $2,000 annually for courses, conferences, and
certifications

Remote-friendly work environment with flexible hours

Mentorship from experienced SREs and engineers on the team

HOW TO APPLY

Submit your resume, a brief cover letter, and a link to your GitHub profile (if available) via our careers
portal at careers.cloudpulse.io. Please apply with the job reference 'JSRE-2024-SF'. We review
applications on a rolling basis.
company: learning how systems work at scale and want hands

Familiarity with observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, or similar platforms
Previous exposure to incident response or on-call environments
Understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure)
